normalize: P1: Two .hero-sub paragraphs use a negative margin hack (margin-top: calc(-1 * var(--space-md))) instead of a proper spacing token — breaks the design system's spacing rhythm
P2: Feature card h3 uses var(--font-body) while pipeline h3 uses var(--font-display) — inconsistent heading treatment across parallel sections
P2: Agent card font-size is hardcoded to 12px instead of using --text-xs token
P3: .coming-soon::before uses CSS content for the 'Soon' badge text — this is presentational text that should be in the HTML for screen readers
harden: P0: ALL 4 external GitHub links return HTTP 404 — the repo github.com/RafayelGardishyan/rafayels-engineering does not exist. This is the single most damaging issue on the page.
P1: No error boundary or fallback if Google Fonts fail to load — potential FOIT (Flash of Invisible Text) with no font-display fallback in the CSS link
P2: document.execCommand('copy') fallback is deprecated and may be removed from browsers
P2: No CSP meta tag or security headers visible in the HTML
P3: Terminal demo animation re-trigger uses offsetHeight reflow hack which can cause layout thrashing
optimize: P1: All CSS (~1200 lines) and JS (~150 lines) are inline in a single 57KB HTML file — should be extracted to cacheable external files
P2: Google Fonts loads 3 font families (6 weights total) in a single request — consider subsetting or self-hosting for performance
P2: SVG noise texture is generated via a data URI in CSS which is re-parsed on every paint — could be a static asset
P3: IntersectionObserver instances (4 separate observers) could be consolidated into fewer observers with shared callbacks
P3: No lazy loading considerations — all content loads at once (though for a single-page landing this is acceptable)
adapt: P1: At 700-900px viewport, pipeline grid transitions from 5→3→2→1 columns but the arrow connectors break between column wraps — arrows point right but content wraps below
P2: No explicit dark/light mode toggle — forces dark theme which may be inaccessible in high-brightness environments
P2: Footer at <900px stacks vertically but footer-links don't re-center, creating an awkward left-aligned stack
P3: Hero stats at <600px wrap but have no visual separation between wrapped rows
P3: No container queries — responsive design relies entirely on viewport-based breakpoints
clarify: P2: 'Knowledge that compounds' in hero is jargon-heavy — unclear to first-time visitors what this means concretely
P2: Pipeline step commands (e.g., /workflows:brainstorm) are shown but never explained — new users won't know these are slash commands
P2: '29 Agents, 22 Skills, 19 Commands' stats in hero lack context — what's the difference between an agent, skill, and command?
P3: 'Changelog' with 'Soon' badge gives no timeline or signup for updates
P3: Terminal demo shows '/re:feature' but the hero section shows the same command — redundant demonstration without progressive disclosure

findings: Nav CTA 'Get Started' has background color change (#3877e0 → #4a88e8) + translateY(-1px) on hover — subtle but effective
Nav links change from --text-dim to --text-bright on hover with 0.2s transition — clean but the color shift is very subtle on the dark background
Hero CTA primary has hover with background change, translateY(-2px), and box-shadow glow — strong affordance
Hero CTA secondary has border-color change + color brightening on hover — weaker affordance than primary (good hierarchy)
Footer links change color to --blue on hover — consistent with the blue accent system
Footer 'Documentation' link shows underline on hover — confirmed visually in screenshot
Pipeline steps, feature cards, and agent cards all have translateY(-2px) lift + border highlight on hover — consistent card hover pattern
Copy buttons have color, border-color, and background transitions on hover
All transitions use 0.15-0.3s durations — appropriately smooth, not sluggish
Focus-visible states present globally with 2px cyan outline + 2px offset — keyboard accessibility covered
The /re:feature code element in hero has a hover glow effect (box-shadow) — nice interactive detail

findings.description: ALL 4 external GitHub links (repo, readme, agents, issues) return HTTP 404. Every outbound link on the page is broken.
findings.recommendation: Create the GitHub repo at the expected URL or update all hrefs to the correct repository URL. This destroys credibility for any visitor who clicks through.

findings.description: Segmentation map reveals nav link spacing inconsistency — the gap between 'Capabilities' and 'Agents' is visually wider than between 'Workflow' and 'Capabilities' due to text length differences with fixed gap value.
findings.recommendation: Consider using min-width on nav items or adjusting gap to account for visual rhythm rather than relying on a single gap token.

findings.description: Missing og:image meta tag — social sharing will show no preview image, severely reducing click-through from Twitter/LinkedIn/Slack.
findings.recommendation: Add an og:image with a 1200×630 branded card showing the blueprint grid aesthetic and tagline.

findings.description: Segmentation map for screenshot-2 shows a large empty 'Content Placeholder (Empty Box)' region above 'Built for Serious Work' — this is the terminal demo area which is off-screen, creating a ~200px dead zone of empty dark space when scrolling.
findings.recommendation: Reduce section padding between pipeline terminal demo and capabilities section, or add a visual bridge element.

findings.description: Hero uses two separate .hero-sub paragraphs joined by a negative margin hack (calc(-1 * var(--space-md))), breaking the established spacing system and creating fragile layout.
findings.recommendation: Consolidate into a single .hero-sub paragraph or use a proper spacing token between them.

findings.description: Google Fonts loaded via link tag with no font-display descriptor in the preload — risks FOIT on slow connections. The font CSS URL uses display=swap but the preload as='style' doesn't guarantee the swap behavior applies before render.
findings.recommendation: Self-host fonts with explicit @font-face declarations using font-display: swap, or add a JS-based font loading strategy.

findings.description: Segmentation map shows 'Visual Inconsistency PrefixAlignment' in the agents section — the '03 — REVIEW AGENTS' prefix label doesn't align with the card grid below it.
findings.recommendation: Ensure section labels align to the same grid as the content below them.

findings.description: Pipeline arrow connectors (::after pseudo-elements) break at responsive breakpoints — at 1100px the 3rd arrow disappears but arrows 4-5 still point right while content has wrapped to a new row.
findings.recommendation: Use CSS :nth-child selectors that match the current grid-template-columns at each breakpoint to show/hide arrows correctly.

findings.description: Hero stats (29 Agents, 22 Skills, 19 Commands, 16 Parallel Reviewers) are presented without context — a new visitor has no mental model for what distinguishes an 'agent' from a 'skill' from a 'command'.
findings.recommendation: Add brief tooltip or descriptive text clarifying each stat, or reduce to 2-3 most impactful numbers.

findings.description: The dark blueprint/grid aesthetic, while well-executed, is a very common pattern for developer tool landing pages (Linear, Vercel, Raycast all use similar themes). Limited brand differentiation.
findings.recommendation: Introduce more distinctive visual elements — the corner marks are a start but the overall feel is generic dark-mode dev-tool. Consider leaning harder into the 'engineering blueprint' metaphor with more unique visual treatments.

findings.description: The 'Changelog' footer item uses a CSS ::before pseudo-element to inject 'Soon' text — this is invisible to screen readers and violates content/presentation separation.
findings.recommendation: Move 'Soon' text into the HTML markup with an appropriate aria-label.

findings.description: The --text-dim color (#b0bcc8) on --bg (#0a0e17) has a contrast ratio of approximately 8.5:1 (good), but the --blue-dim (rgba(56,119,224,0.3)) used for borders and accents nearly disappears on the dark background.
findings.recommendation: Increase border opacity from 0.3 to 0.4-0.5 for better visual definition of card boundaries.

findings.description: All CSS and JS are inline in a 57KB HTML file with no external caching. Repeat visitors must re-download all styles and scripts.
findings.recommendation: Extract CSS and JS to separate files with content-hash filenames for long-term caching.

findings.description: Feature card headings use --font-body (Schibsted Grotesk) at font-weight: 600 while pipeline step headings use --font-display (Instrument Serif) at font-weight: 400 — inconsistent h3 treatment between visually parallel sections.
findings.recommendation: Standardize h3 treatment or make the distinction intentional and more pronounced.

findings.description: Terminal demo animation has fixed delays that only fire once — if a user scrolls past quickly they may miss the animation entirely, and scrolling back won't replay it (observer unobserves after trigger).
findings.recommendation: Allow terminal animation to replay on re-entry, or ensure the final state is visible immediately as a fallback.

findings.description: Footer at mobile breakpoint (<900px) stacks vertically but the footer-links remain left-aligned, creating asymmetric visual weight.
findings.recommendation: Center footer-links at mobile breakpoint or use text-align: center on the entire footer.

summary: A competent dark-mode developer tool landing page with solid foundational craft — proper semantic HTML, ARIA labels, focus-visible states, reduced motion support, print stylesheet, and smooth scroll-triggered animations. However, the single most damaging issue is that ALL external links (GitHub repo, docs, issues) return 404, which completely undermines the page's credibility and call-to-action. The blueprint/grid aesthetic is well-executed but generic for the dev-tool space. Typography and color systems are cohesive, and the responsive breakpoints are thoughtfully handled with a few edge-case issues. Score of 68 reflects strong technical foundations dragged down significantly by broken links and a few layout/spacing inconsistencies visible in the segmentation maps.
